clusters.root_cause_summary: The stair centroid lies in a navmesh-disconnected component — one upstair (q3zU7Yy5E5s) and one downstair (qyAac8rV8Zk) — causing Reach_stair_centroid to remain False on every attempt; pointnav never converges, get_close_to_stair consumes the episode budget, and no floor switch occurs.
clusters.cross_scene_pattern: Identical: 35+ consecutive Reach_stair_centroid:False in both scenes; stair frontier disabled at least twice without a floor switch; candidate_1 premature_stair_climb_success_guard patch produces zero behavioral change (mode fingerprints identical across candidates) because the stair-climb phase is never entered. Different: q3zU7Yy5E5s targets an upstair centroid with a 75-step regression introduced by the patch (381→456 steps) due to wasted centroid-approach budget; qyAac8rV8Zk targets a downstair centroid and exhibits min_dis_to_downstair oscillating 160–166 px with no convergence, and its episode length is unchanged by the patch.
clusters.transfer_notes: Applying the premature_stair_climb_success_guard to both scenes demonstrated conclusively that any intervention inside the stair-climb state machine (_process_stair_climb_stats) is off-causal-path when the centroid approach phase never succeeds. The 75-step regression in q3zU7Yy5E5s also teaches that naive parameter perturbations can shift internal sampling state enough to change centroid coordinates ([-2.12→-2.10] upstair) without changing reachability — confirming the root cause is topological, not metric.
clusters.highest_leverage_untested: stair_centroid_navmesh_reachability_precheck_before_get_close_to_stair: before committing to get_close_to_stair, run a navmesh pathfinding query from the robot's current position to the stair centroid; if no path exists, immediately fall back to alternative_stair_entry_point_sampling_from_connected_navmesh_boundary (sample the nearest reachable navmesh vertex within the stair bounding box) or trigger early_abort_on_N_consecutive_centroid_failures_with_floor_skip. This addresses both the upstair and downstair variants with a single guard.

clusters.root_cause_summary: The stair is successfully physically traversed (floor_step resets to 0 at step 198), but the goal (bed) lies in a navmesh-disconnected region of the upper floor; post-switch frontier supply exhausts in ~16 steps (only two frontiers at 0.2m and 2.2m), and five floor_reinits plus two NOQUIT rescues all fail to reach the goal.
clusters.cross_scene_pattern: Single-scene cluster. Distinguishing signature: stair climb succeeds (T5_STAIR_CLIMB_EVAL SUCCESS, paused_step=20) yet the episode still fails; the premature_stair_climb_success_guard patch (candidate_1) was off-target because SUCCESS already fires at paused_step=20 > MIN_STAIR_STEPS=15, yielding bit-for-bit identical logs. The failure is entirely post-floor-switch: sparse frontier seeding on the upper floor plus navmesh isolation of the goal region means repeated re-exploration attempts cannot reach the bed.
clusters.transfer_notes: The stair_centroid_navmesh_disconnection cluster's fixes (precheck before get_close_to_stair, alternative entry point sampling) do not transfer here — the stair was already climbed. The key cross-cluster insight is the inverse: succeeding at floor traversal is necessary but not sufficient; ASCENT currently has no post-switch check for whether the goal region on the new floor is navmesh-reachable. A navmesh_connectivity_precheck_for_floor_skip (skip a floor entirely if the goal is unreachable on it) would also help this cluster by avoiding wasted post-switch exploration budget.
clusters.highest_leverage_untested: post_floor_switch_frontier_seeding_radius: after a confirmed floor switch, expand the initial frontier seeding radius to span a significantly larger portion of the new floor's connected navmesh components, so post-switch exploration is not limited to the two nearest frontiers (0.2m and 2.2m). This should be combined with navmesh_connectivity_precheck_for_floor_skip to detect and preemptively skip floors where the goal object lies in a disconnected component, redirecting exploration budget to reachable floors.

forbidden_moves.reason: LLM response parsing is off-causal-path in both scenes: the floor switch never occurs so any LLM ranking of upper-floor frontiers is irrelevant; ruled out in both q3zU7Yy5E5s and qyAac8rV8Zk.
Globally ruled out; ruled out in XB4GS9ShBRE explicitly.
LLM prompt tuning cannot affect navmesh topology; ruled out in both cluster scenes.
Globally ruled out; ruled out in XB4GS9ShBRE.

Globally ruled out; ruled out in XB4GS9ShBRE.
LLM trigger threshold cannot repair a disconnected navmesh centroid; ruled out in both cluster scenes.
Globally ruled out; ruled out in XB4GS9ShBRE.
Multi-floor LLM path is never reached when floor switch never occurs; ruled out in both cluster scenes.
Globally ruled out; ruled out in XB4GS9ShBRE.
Floor switch interval gating is irrelevant when the stair approach itself fails; ruled out in both cluster scenes.
Globally ruled out; ruled out in XB4GS9ShBRE.

Candidate_1 applied this guard to both scenes; identical behavioral fingerprints and zero SR change confirm the stair-climb phase (_process_stair_climb_stats) is never entered when Reach_stair_centroid is always False — the guard is structurally unreachable for this failure class.

Candidate_1 applied this guard to XB4GS9ShBRE; it had zero effect because SUCCESS already fires at paused_step=20 which exceeds MIN_STAIR_STEPS=15 — the guard threshold was already satisfied and never activated.

Stair carrot waypoint distance (look_for_downstair mode) is irrelevant when get_close_to_stair fails before any stair-climb carrot navigation begins; ruled out explicitly for q3zU7Yy5E5s (upstair case).
Stair carrot distance is irrelevant post-traversal; the stair was already successfully climbed in XB4GS9ShBRE. Ruled out explicitly.
Frontier scoring cannot affect centroid reachability; ruled out explicitly for qyAac8rV8Zk (downstair case) — the agent never returns to frontier-based exploration once stuck in get_close_to_stair.
